projects.title: Cybaashcloud
projects.desc:
CYBAASH — Autonomous Cyber Range Portfolio


A production-grade cybersecurity portfolio that doesn't just talk security — it practices it. Every layer is engineered with an attacker's mindset.

Built an 8-module SOC tracker covering visitor fingerprinting, behavioral anomaly detection, honeypot link injection, URL scanning, rate abuse tracking, and async log relay to a Cloudflare Worker backend — completely isolated from the admin interface by design.

Integrated CYBAASH AI (Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite) through a rate-limited Cloudflare Worker proxy — API keys never exposed client-side, with geo-blocking, CORS enforcement, and graceful offline fallback.

Deployed an 11-step CI/CD pipeline: JS syntax checks, JSON schema validation, SOC assertions, HTML integrity scanning, cert data validation, Worker security audits, secret leak scanning via Gitleaks, and performance budget enforcement on every push.

Admin CMS uses SHA-256 hashed auth — no plaintext passwords, no default credentials. All changes committed via GitHub API, creating a full audit trail.

Real-world pentest work documented: MySQL port 3306 public exposure (CVSS 9.8), XML-RPC brute force bypass, REST API admin enumeration, backup config file exposure, and missing security headers — each with CVSS scoring, attack chain analysis, and remediation guidance.

 · AWS · Cisco · Microsoft · Penetration Testing · AI Red Teaming · Cloud Security

projects.title: Black Box web application Pen Test Report for health care enterprise
projects.desc:
Black Box Web Application Penetration Test — Healthcare Sector
Conducted: March 2026 · Methodology: OWASP · Type: Black Box

Performed a full black box web application security assessment against a live production healthcare website hosted on shared infrastructure. No prior credentials, no source code access — pure external attacker simulation from first scan to final report.

Identified 9 vulnerabilities across Critical, High, Medium, and Low severity bands with full CVSS scoring, evidence, impact analysis, and prioritized remediation guidance.

Critical findings:
→ MySQL port 3306 publicly exposed (CVSS 9.8) — database directly reachable from the internet, accepting unauthenticated connection attempts from any IP
→ xmlrpc.php fully enabled (CVSS 9.1) — system.multicall method allowing thousands of login attempts per request, completely bypassing rate limiting

High findings:
→ Admin username enumerated via unauthenticated REST API endpoint
→ Backup wp-config files present on server containing plaintext DB credentials

Documented a complete 4-step attack chain: username enumeration → xmlrpc brute force → authenticated web shell upload → full database dump — demonstrating how individual medium-severity misconfigurations combine into a critical compromise path.

Additional findings: missing security headers (X-Frame-Options, CSP, HSTS), PHP and server version disclosure, SSL mismatch, WordPress version exposure, and legacy FTP port open.

All testing was non-destructive. No data modified, exfiltrated, or brute forced against live credentials.

Tools: Nmap · WPScan · curl · Manual testing · CVSS v3.1 scoring

flags.desc: Exploit a Rick & Morty themed vulnerable web server. Find 3 secret ingredients hidden across the machine using web enumeration, command injection, and Linux privilege escalation.
